tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto for early trip shopping
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has traditional optical districts — could you buy glasses walking distance from your first hotel?
tasks.reward: Locks in your first Japan purchase and saves time on day 1
nuggets: Kyoto optical shops cluster near Shijo-Kawaramachi
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find eyeglasses stores in Kyoto, Japan. I'm arriving March 23, 2026 and want to buy 4 pairs of glasses early in the trip. I need stores with: good selection of frames, English-speaking staff (helpful but not required), ability to process prescriptions quickly (ideally same-day or next-day). I'm staying in central Kyoto. Give me a list of 5-7 recommended stores with addresses, Google Maps links, opening hours, and notes about specialties or price ranges.

tasks.task_name: Validate insurance/prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of glasses
tasks.first_domino: Email your insurance: subject 'International glasses purchase coverage'
tasks.hook_line: 6 days out — if insurance needs pre-authorization or won't cover international purchases, you need to know now.
tasks.reward: Avoids a $1200 surprise and confirms whether you can buy all 4 pairs
nuggets: Some plans reimburse international with receipts
Prescription copy needed - check expiration date
tasks.system_prompt: Help me figure out what I need to buy 4 pairs of eyeglasses in Japan. I have vision insurance in the US (unknown plan details yet). I need to know: (1) Will my US insurance reimburse international glasses purchases? (2) What documentation do I need from the store (itemized receipts, prescription details)? (3) Do I need to bring my prescription or can Japanese opticians write a new one? (4) Are there limits on how many pairs insurance covers per year? Give me a checklist of things to verify with my insurance company and what to ask the Japanese optical store.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps, create new list 'Kyoto Must-See'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples, bamboo groves, hidden sake bars, and the best ramen in Japan — which ones are actually worth waking up early for?
tasks.reward: Turns 2.5 blank days into a concrete itinerary with walking routes and food stops
nuggets: Fushimi Inari free 24hr access, crowds thin after 6pm
Arashiyama bamboo grove best before 9am weekdays
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 2.5 days in Kyoto (March 23-25, 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, nature walks, local food, and hidden spots more than tourist crowds. Already staying in central Kyoto. I want a day-by-day itinerary with: (1) 3-4 main activities per day with opening hours and entry fees, (2) suggested walking routes between locations with Google Maps links, (3) breakfast/lunch/dinner recommendations near each cluster of activities, (4) mix of iconic landmarks (Fushimi Inari, Kinkaku-ji) and lesser-known gems, (5) realistic timing that accounts for travel and meals. We don't mind early mornings to beat crowds.

tasks.first_domino: Search 'Takayama day trips hiking' on Google
tasks.hook_line: Takayama is the Japanese Alps base camp — old town morning markets, mountain trails, and sake breweries all in one small city.
tasks.reward: Closes the Takayama planning gap — only Tokyo left after this
nuggets: Takayama morning market 7am-noon, best produce selection
Shirakawa-go village 50min bus, UNESCO World Heritage
Hida beef rivals Kobe, try at local yakiniku spots
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 2 days in Takayama, Japan (March 26-27, 2026). Traveling with Dana. We like hiking, traditional architecture, local food, and small-town vibes. Already have hotel booked. I want: (1) best day hike within 1 hour of Takayama (moderate difficulty, 3-5 hours, scenic), (2) old town walking route with sake breweries and morning market timing, (3) whether Shirakawa-go village day trip is worth it or if we should focus on Takayama itself, (4) dinner spots for Hida beef, (5) any hot springs or onsen worth visiting. Give me a 2-day itinerary with realistic timing and transport info.

tasks.first_domino: List Tokyo neighborhoods you want to explore (Shibuya, Shinjuku, Asakusa...)
tasks.hook_line: Tokyo is the final piece — 5 days to fill, and this completes your entire Japan itinerary.
tasks.reward: Full trip planned from arrival to departure, ready to go in 6 days
nuggets: Tsukiji Outer Market breakfast 6am-1pm, sushi spots
TeamLab Borderless digital art museum, timed entry
Nakano Broadway for anime/manga, less touristy than Akihabara
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 5 days in Tokyo (March 28 - April 1, 2026). Traveling with Dana. We like: neighborhoods with local character, modern architecture, street food, quirky shops, and mix of traditional/contemporary. Not interested in: Disney, heavy tourist traps, nightclubs. Already have hotel in central Tokyo. I want a day-by-day plan with: (1) 1-2 neighborhoods to explore each day, (2) key landmarks or experiences in each area (temples, parks, markets, observation decks), (3) food recommendations by neighborhood, (4) fountain pen shopping (looking for Sailor limited editions), (5) one day trip outside Tokyo if there's something unmissable. Include realistic walking routes, opening hours, and transport between areas.

tasks.first_domino: Clone the Cerebro repo and open README.md
tasks.hook_line: Developer portal that's never run on your machine — can you untangle the setup without asking for help?
tasks.reward: Unblocks all future Cerebro work and gives you a local playground to explore the codebase
nuggets: Check if Docker required or native Node setup
Might need VPN or internal auth tokens
tasks.system_prompt: Help me get the Cerebro developer portal running on my local machine. I haven't touched this repo in weeks. I need to: (1) identify all dependencies (Docker, Node version, databases, external services), (2) find and configure required environment variables and secrets, (3) troubleshoot common setup errors (port conflicts, missing credentials, network access), (4) verify it's actually running by accessing the UI and making a test API call. Walk me through the setup step-by-step, and help debug any errors that come up. Assume I have standard dev tools installed (Node, Docker, VS Code) but no project-specific config.

recommended_slots.reason: Weekend ramp-up energy, low effort research task, unblocks insurance validation, 6 days to departure
Quick coordination win before Monday, prevents costly surprise, pairs well with slot 1
Curiosity-driven research, batchable with other Japan planning, can work in short bursts
